{{Battlebox | battle_name = 中先代の乱 | campaign = 延元の乱 |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = [[建武の乱]]（延元の乱） | date = [[1335年]]（[[建武 (日本)|建武]]2年）[[7月 (旧暦)|7月]] | place = 関東周辺（[[武蔵国]]、[[下野国]]、[[駿河国]]、[[遠江国]]、[[相模国]]） | result = 旧鎌倉幕府軍の鎌倉撤退 | combatant1 = [[ファイル:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[足利氏]] | combatant2 = [[ファイル:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[鎌倉幕府|旧鎌倉幕府軍]] | commander1 = [[ファイル:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[足利尊氏]]
[[ファイル:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[足利直義]] | commander2 = [[ファイル:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[北条時行]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]][[諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)|諏訪頼重]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''中先代の乱'''（なかせんだいのらん）は、[[1335年]]（[[建武 (日本)|建武]]2年）[[7月 (旧暦)|7月]]、[[北条高時]]（[[鎌倉幕府]]第14代[[執権]]）の遺児[[北条時行]]が、[[御内人]]の[[諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)|諏訪頼重]]らに擁立され、鎌倉幕府再興のため挙兵した反乱。北条時行は僅か10歳（諸説あり）の少年であったが[[足利直義]]を破り、鎌倉奪還に成功した。先代（[[北条氏]]）と後代（[[足利氏]]）との間にあって、一時的に鎌倉を支配したことから中先代の乱と呼ばれている。また、鎌倉支配が20日余りしか続かなかったことから、'''廿日先代'''（はつかせんだい）の異名もある{{Sfn|三浦|2008}}。 == 概要 == 鎌倉幕府滅亡後、[[建武の新政]]により、鎌倉には、後醍醐天皇の皇子・[[成良親王]]を長とし尊氏の弟の[[足利直義]]が執権としてこれを補佐する形の[[鎌倉将軍府]]が設置された。しかし建武政権は武家の支持を得られず、北条一族の残党などは各地で蜂起を繰り返していた。北条氏が守護を務めていた[[信濃国]]もその1つで、千曲川（[[信濃川]]）周辺ではたびたび蜂起が繰り返され、足利方の[[守護]][[小笠原貞宗]]らが鎮圧にあたっていた{{Sfn|阪田|2012|p=9}}。 1335年（建武2年）[[6月 (旧暦)|6月]]には、鎌倉時代に[[関東申次]]を務め、北条氏と繋がりがあった[[公家]]・[[西園寺公宗]]らが[[京都]]に潜伏していた北条高時の弟[[北条泰家]]（時興）を匿い、[[持明院統]]の[[光厳上皇]]を擁立して政権転覆を企てた陰謀が発覚する。公宗らは後醍醐天皇の[[暗殺]]に失敗して誅殺されたが、泰家は逃れ、各地の北条残党に挙兵を呼びかけた。 信濃に潜伏していた北条時行は、[[御内人]]であった[[諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)|諏訪頼重]]や[[滋野氏]]・[[仁科氏]]らに擁立されて挙兵した『[[梅松論]]』。時行の信濃挙兵に応じて[[北陸地方|北陸]]では北条一族の[[名越時兼]]が挙兵する。諏訪氏の縁続きに当たる保科弥三郎（[[保科氏]]）や四宮左衛門太郎（武水別神社神官家）、関屋氏ら時行勢は[[青沼合戦]]において[[船山守護所]]（小笠原貞宗）を襲撃した。この間に[[諏訪氏]]・滋野氏らは信濃国衙を焼き討ち襲撃して、建武政権が任命した公家の国司（[[清原氏 (広澄流)|清原]][[真人]]某）を自害させる『[[太平記]]』。 ところが、京都の建武政権は当初、反乱軍が時行を擁しているとの情報を掴んでいなかったらしく同年7月に信濃の豪族であった市河助房が京都に充てた[[着到状]]（[[市河文書]]所収「建武二年七月日市河助房着到状」）には、青沼合戦など反乱軍の動向については記されているが、反乱軍そのものについては「諏訪祝并滋野一族等依企謀叛」と記されており、時行の存在は知られていなかった。、京都では反乱軍は[[木曽谷|木曽路]]から[[尾張国]]に抜け、最終的には政権のある京都へと向かうと予想したために鎌倉将軍府への連絡が遅れ、それが後の鎌倉陥落につながったとみられている{{Sfn|阪田|2012|p=10}}。 勢いに乗った時行軍は[[武蔵国]]へ入り鎌倉に向けて進軍する。7月20日頃に女影原（[[埼玉県]][[日高市]]）で[[渋川義季]]や[[岩松経家]]らが率いる鎌倉将軍府の軍を、小手指ヶ原（同県[[所沢市]]）で[[今川範満 (南北朝時代)|今川範満]]の軍を、武蔵府中で救援に駆けつけた[[下野国]]守護・[[小山秀朝]]の軍を打ち破り、これらを自害あるいは討死させた。続いて、井手の沢（[[東京都]][[町田市]]）にて鎌倉から出陣して時行軍を迎撃した[[足利直義]]をも破る。直義は尊氏の子の幼い[[足利義詮]]や、後醍醐天皇の皇子[[成良親王]]らを連れて鎌倉を逃れる。鎌倉には建武政権から失脚した後醍醐天皇の皇子[[護良親王]]（前征夷大将軍）が幽閉されていたが、直義は鎌倉を落ちる際に密かに家臣の[[淵辺義博]]に護良親王を殺害させている（7月23日）。鎌倉に護良を将軍・時行を執権とする鎌倉幕府が再興され建武政権に対抗する存在になることを恐れていたからと考えられている{{Sfn|阪田|2012|p=10}}。24日は鶴見（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[鶴見区 (横浜市)|鶴見区]]）にて鎌倉将軍府側は最後の抵抗を試みるが[[佐竹義直 (南北朝時代)|佐竹義直]]（[[佐竹貞義]]の子）らが戦死、翌25日に時行は鎌倉に入り、一時的に支配する。更に時行勢は逃げる直義を[[駿河国]]手越河原で撃破した。直義は8月2日に[[三河国]]矢作に拠点を構え、乱の報告を京都に伝えると同時に成良親王を返還している。 (藤沢市)|辻堂]]で敗れた諏訪頼重が鎌倉[[勝長寿院]]で自害して、時行は鎌倉を保つこと20日余りで逃亡する。 後醍醐天皇は尊氏へ出陣の許可は与えなかったものの、8月30日の[[小山朝氏]]への[[下野国]]司兼守護への補任は尊氏の奏請に応じたものと考えられ、また間に合わなかったとは言え九州の[[大友貞載]]に出陣を求める[[綸旨]]を出していることから、少なくてもこの段階では尊氏と天皇の方針に大きな違いはなかったと考えられている。ところが、9月27日になり、尊氏は鎌倉において、乱の鎮圧に付き従った将士に勝手に恩賞の分配を行うため[[袖判下文]]を発給し、建武政権の上洛命令を無視したりするなど、建武政権から離反する（[[延元の乱]]）{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=12-13}}。 北条時行に従った武士には名越氏のような北条氏一門もいたが、大部分が諏訪頼重のような御内人であった。彼らの中には千葉氏・宇都宮氏・三浦氏などの関東有力武家の庶流の出身者が多数含まれており、結果的に関東武士が多数含まれることになった。これに対して佐竹氏・小山氏などの関東の御家人が鎌倉将軍府側に加わり、更に既に将軍府に出仕していた旧幕府吏僚や御内人の多くも時行の動きには従わずに、直義とともに鎌倉から脱出している。このため、鎌倉に入った時行は公式の幕府文書を発給することが出来ず、御内人のみで出せる得宗家の奉行人連署奉書で命令を下している。そして、建武政権に仕えていた旧幕府吏僚や御内人の中にも尊氏とともに時行討伐に参加する者がおり、時行および御内人の挙兵は結果的には御内人同士の戦いとなって鎌倉幕府再興の可能性を失わせるとともに、[[室町幕府]]や[[鎌倉府]]を支える吏僚層を形成するきっかけとなった{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=12-18}}{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=23-24}}。 {{Battlebox | battle_name = 中先代の乱 | campaign = 延元の乱 |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = [[建武の乱]]（延元の乱） | date = [[建武 (日本)|建武]]2年（[[1335年]]）[[7月 (旧暦)|7月]] | place = 関東周辺（[[武蔵国]]、[[下野国]]、[[駿河国]]、[[遠江国]]、[[相模国]]） | result = 旧鎌倉幕府軍の鎌倉撤退 | combatant1 = [[ファイル:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[足利氏]] | combatant2 = [[ファイル:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[鎌倉幕府|旧鎌倉幕府軍]] | commander1 = [[ファイル:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[足利尊氏]]
[[ファイル:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[足利直義]] | commander2 = [[ファイル:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[北条時行]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]][[諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)|諏訪頼重]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''中先代の乱'''（なかせんだいのらん）は、[[建武 (日本)|建武]]2年（[[1335年]]）[[7月 (旧暦)|7月]]、[[北条高時]]（[[鎌倉幕府]]第14代[[執権]]）の遺児[[北条時行]]が、[[御内人]]の[[諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)|諏訪頼重]]らに擁立され、鎌倉幕府再興のため挙兵した反乱。北条時行は僅か10歳（諸説あり）の少年であったが[[足利直義]]を破り、鎌倉奪還に成功した。先代（[[北条氏]]）と後代（[[足利氏]]）との間にあって、一時的に鎌倉を支配したことから中先代の乱と呼ばれている。また、鎌倉支配が20日余りしか続かなかったことから、'''廿日先代'''（はつかせんだい）の異名もある{{Sfn|三浦|2008}}。 == 経過 == 鎌倉幕府滅亡後、[[建武の新政]]により、鎌倉には、後醍醐天皇の皇子・[[成良親王]]を長とし尊氏の弟の[[足利直義]]が執権としてこれを補佐する形の[[鎌倉将軍府]]が設置された。しかし建武政権は武家の支持を得られず、北条一族の残党などは各地で蜂起を繰り返していた。北条氏が守護を務めていた[[信濃国]]もその1つで、千曲川（[[信濃川]]）周辺ではたびたび蜂起が繰り返され、足利方の[[守護]][[小笠原貞宗]]らが鎮圧にあたっていた{{Sfn|阪田|2012|p=9}}。 [[建武 (日本)|建武]]2年（[[1335年]]）[[6月 (旧暦)|6月]]には、鎌倉時代に[[関東申次]]を務め、北条氏と繋がりがあった[[公家]]・[[西園寺公宗]]らが[[京都]]に潜伏していた北条高時の弟[[北条泰家]]（時興）を匿い、[[持明院統]]の[[光厳上皇]]を擁立して政権転覆を企てた陰謀が発覚する。公宗らは後醍醐天皇の[[暗殺]]に失敗して誅殺されたが、泰家は逃れ、各地の北条残党に挙兵を呼びかけた。 信濃に潜伏していた北条時行は、[[御内人]]であった[[諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)|諏訪頼重]]や[[滋野氏]]・[[仁科氏]]らに擁立されて挙兵した『[[梅松論]]』。時行の信濃挙兵に応じて[[北陸地方|北陸]]では北条一族の[[名越時兼]]が挙兵する。諏訪氏の縁続きに当たる保科弥三郎（[[保科氏]]）や四宮左衛門太郎（武水別神社神官家）、関屋氏ら時行勢は[[青沼合戦]]において[[船山守護所]]（小笠原貞宗）を襲撃した。この間に[[諏訪氏]]・滋野氏らは信濃国衙を焼き討ち襲撃して、建武政権が任命した公家の国司（[[清原氏 (広澄流)|清原]][[真人]]某）を自害させる『[[太平記]]』。 ところが、京都の建武政権は当初、反乱軍が時行を擁しているとの情報を掴んでいなかったらしく同年7月に信濃の豪族であった市河助房が京都に充てた[[着到状]]（[[市河文書]]所収「建武二年七月日市河助房着到状」）には、青沼合戦など反乱軍の動向については記されているが、反乱軍そのものについては「諏訪祝并滋野一族等依企謀叛」と記されており、時行の存在は知られていなかった。、京都では反乱軍は[[木曽谷|木曽路]]から[[尾張国]]に抜け、最終的には政権のある京都へと向かうと予想したために鎌倉将軍府への連絡が遅れ、それが後の鎌倉陥落につながったとみられている{{Sfn|阪田|2012|p=10}}。 勢いに乗った時行軍は[[武蔵国]]へ入り鎌倉に向けて進軍する。7月20日頃に女影原（[[埼玉県]][[日高市]]）で[[渋川義季]]や[[岩松経家]]らが率いる鎌倉将軍府の軍を、小手指ヶ原（同県[[所沢市]]）で[[今川範満 (南北朝時代)|今川範満]]の軍を、武蔵府中で救援に駆けつけた[[下野国]]守護・[[小山秀朝]]の軍を打ち破り、これらを自害あるいは討死させた。続いて、井手の沢（[[東京都]][[町田市]]）にて鎌倉から出陣して時行軍を迎撃した[[足利直義]]をも破る。直義は尊氏の子の幼い[[足利義詮]]や、後醍醐天皇の皇子[[成良親王]]らを連れて鎌倉を逃れる。 鎌倉には建武政権から失脚した後醍醐天皇の皇子[[護良親王]]（前征夷大将軍）が幽閉されていたが、直義は鎌倉を落ちる際に密かに家臣の[[淵辺義博]]に護良親王を殺害させている（7月23日）。鎌倉に護良を将軍・時行を執権とする鎌倉幕府が再興され建武政権に対抗する存在になることを恐れていたからと考えられている{{Sfn|阪田|2012|p=10}}。 7月24日、鎌倉将軍府側は鶴見（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[鶴見区 (横浜市)|鶴見区]]）にて最後の抵抗を試みるが、[[佐竹義直 (南北朝時代)|佐竹義直]]（[[佐竹貞義]]の子）らが戦死、翌25日に時行は鎌倉に入り、一時的に支配する。更に時行勢は逃げる直義を、[[駿河国]]手越河原で撃破した。直義は乱の報告を京都に伝えると同時に成良親王を返還し、8月2日に[[三河国]]矢作に拠点を構えた。 時行勢の侵攻を知らされた建武政権では、足利尊氏が後醍醐天皇に対して、時行討伐の許可と同時に武家政権の設立に必要となる総追捕使と[[征夷大将軍]]の役職を要請するが、後醍醐天皇は要請を拒否する。 8月2日、尊氏は勅状を得ないまま出陣し、後醍醐天皇は尊氏に追って征東将軍の号を与える。尊氏は直義と合流し、9日に[[遠江国]]橋本（[[静岡県]][[湖西市]]）、12日に[[小夜の中山]]にて尊氏と同道してきた[[今川頼国]]の手により[[名越高邦]]戦死、14日に駿河国の[[清見関]]および国衙にて諏訪小二郎金刺頼秀（諏訪大社下社社家）が戦死、17日に[[相模国]][[箱根]]、18日に相模国[[相模川]]で尊氏方は義経来の北条方に遺恨を持つ[[中村経長]]が獅子奮迅の働きをするも中村沿革「中村郷土史」p.15.今川頼国・[[今川頼周|頼周]]兄弟が戦死するなど各地が激戦に見舞われた。時行勢は次第に劣勢となり戦線は徐々に後退。19日には相模国[[辻堂地区 (藤沢市)|辻堂]]で敗れた諏訪頼重が鎌倉[[勝長寿院]]で自害して、時行は鎌倉を保つこと20日余りで逃亡する。 後醍醐天皇は尊氏へ出陣の許可は与えなかったものの、8月30日の[[小山朝氏]]への[[下野国]]司兼守護への補任は尊氏の奏請に応じたものと考えられ、また間に合わなかったとは言え九州の[[大友貞載]]に出陣を求める[[綸旨]]を出していることから、少なくてもこの段階では尊氏と天皇の方針に大きな違いはなかったと考えられている。ところが、9月27日になり、尊氏は鎌倉において、乱の鎮圧に付き従った将士に勝手に恩賞の分配を行うため[[袖判下文]]を発給し、建武政権の上洛命令を無視したりするなど、建武政権から離反する（[[延元の乱]]）{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=12-13}}。 北条時行に従った武士には名越氏のような北条氏一門もいたが、大部分が諏訪頼重のような御内人であった。彼らの中には千葉氏・宇都宮氏・三浦氏などの関東有力武家の庶流の出身者が多数含まれており、結果的に関東武士が多数含まれることになった。これに対して佐竹氏・小山氏などの関東の御家人が鎌倉将軍府側に加わり、更に既に将軍府に出仕していた旧幕府吏僚や御内人の多くも時行の動きには従わずに、直義とともに鎌倉から脱出している。このため、鎌倉に入った時行は公式の幕府文書を発給することが出来ず、御内人のみで出せる得宗家の奉行人連署奉書で命令を下している。そして、建武政権に仕えていた旧幕府吏僚や御内人の中にも尊氏とともに時行討伐に参加する者がおり、時行および御内人の挙兵は結果的には御内人同士の戦いとなって鎌倉幕府再興の可能性を失わせるとともに、[[室町幕府]]や[[鎌倉府]]を支える吏僚層を形成するきっかけとなった{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=12-18}}{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=23-24}}。 時行は鎌倉を逃れた後も各地に潜伏し、南北朝成立後は吉野の[[南朝 (日本)|南朝]]から[[朝敵]]免除の[[綸旨]]を受けて南朝に従い、[[新田氏]]や[[北畠顕家]]の軍などに属して足利方と戦うが、[[正平 (日本)|正平]]7年/[[文和]]元年（[[1352年]]）足利方に捕縛され、翌年、鎌倉において処刑されたとされる。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|author=池永二郎|chapter=中先代の乱|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|volume=10|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1989|isbn=978-4-642-00510-4}} * {{Citation|和書|author=小川信|chapter=中先代の乱|title=[[日本史大事典]]|volume=5|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1993|isbn=978-4-582-13105-5}} * {{Citation|和書|author=森茂暁|chapter=中先代の乱|title=[[日本歴史大事典]]|volume=3|publisher=[[小学館]]|year=2001|isbn=978-4-09-523003-0}} * {{Citation|和書|last=三浦|first=紀子|chapter=中先代の乱|title=日本中世史事典|publisher=[[朝倉書店]]|year=2008|isbn=978-4-254-53015-5}} * {{Citation|和書|last=阪田|first=雄一|chapter=中先代の乱と鎌倉将軍府|editor=佐藤博信|title=関東足利氏と東国社会|series=中世東国論:5|publisher=岩田書院|year=2012|isbn=978-4-87294-740-3}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本史の出来事一覧]] * [[日本の合戦一覧]] * [[観応の擾乱]] * [[青沼合戦]] == 外部リンク == * {{Kotobank}} {{デフォルトソート:なかせんたいのらん}} [[Category:日本の南北朝時代の戦い]] [[Category:相模国|戦なかせんたい]] [[Category:鎌倉市の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国|戦なかせんたい]] [[Category:長野県の歴史]] [[Category:1335年の日本]] revision.comment: 「題材にした作品」を設定、『中先代の乱、始末』『逃げ上手の若君』を記載。

7月24日、鎌倉将軍府側は鶴見（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[鶴見区 (横浜市)|鶴見区]]）にて最後の抵抗を試みるが、[[佐竹義直 (南北朝時代)|佐竹義直]]（[[佐竹貞義]]の子）らが戦死、翌25日に時行は鎌倉に入り、一時的に支配する。更に時行勢は逃げる直義を、[[駿河国]]手越河原で撃破した。直義は乱の報告を京都に伝えると同時に成良親王を返還し、8月2日に[[三河国]]矢作に拠点を構えた。 時行勢の侵攻を知らされた建武政権では、足利尊氏が後醍醐天皇に対して、時行討伐の許可と同時に武家政権の設立に必要となる総追捕使と[[征夷大将軍]]の役職を要請するが、後醍醐天皇は要請を拒否する。 8月2日、尊氏は勅状を得ないまま出陣し、後醍醐天皇は尊氏に追って征東将軍の号を与える。尊氏は直義と合流し、9日に[[遠江国]]橋本（[[静岡県]][[湖西市]]）、12日に[[小夜の中山]]にて尊氏と同道してきた[[今川頼国]]の手により[[名越高邦]]戦死、14日に駿河国の[[清見関]]および国衙にて諏訪小二郎金刺頼秀（諏訪大社下社社家）が戦死、17日に[[相模国]][[箱根]]、18日に相模国[[相模川]]で尊氏方は義経来の北条方に遺恨を持つ[[中村経長]]が獅子奮迅の働きをするも中村沿革「中村郷土史」p.15.今川頼国・[[今川頼周|頼周]]兄弟が戦死するなど各地が激戦に見舞われた。時行勢は次第に劣勢となり戦線は徐々に後退。19日には相模国[[辻堂地区 (藤沢市)|辻堂]]で敗れた諏訪頼重が鎌倉[[勝長寿院]]で自害して、時行は鎌倉を保つこと20日余りで逃亡する。 後醍醐天皇は尊氏へ出陣の許可は与えなかったものの、8月30日の[[小山朝氏]]への[[下野国]]司兼守護への補任は尊氏の奏請に応じたものと考えられ、また間に合わなかったとは言え九州の[[大友貞載]]に出陣を求める[[綸旨]]を出していることから、少なくてもこの段階では尊氏と天皇の方針に大きな違いはなかったと考えられている。ところが、9月27日になり、尊氏は鎌倉において、乱の鎮圧に付き従った将士に勝手に恩賞の分配を行うため[[袖判下文]]を発給し、建武政権の上洛命令を無視したりするなど、建武政権から離反する（[[延元の乱]]）{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=12-13}}。 北条時行に従った武士には名越氏のような北条氏一門もいたが、大部分が諏訪頼重のような御内人であった。彼らの中には千葉氏・宇都宮氏・三浦氏などの関東有力武家の庶流の出身者が多数含まれており、結果的に関東武士が多数含まれることになった。これに対して佐竹氏・小山氏などの関東の御家人が鎌倉将軍府側に加わり、更に既に将軍府に出仕していた旧幕府吏僚や御内人の多くも時行の動きには従わずに、直義とともに鎌倉から脱出している。このため、鎌倉に入った時行は公式の幕府文書を発給することが出来ず、御内人のみで出せる得宗家の奉行人連署奉書で命令を下している。そして、建武政権に仕えていた旧幕府吏僚や御内人の中にも尊氏とともに時行討伐に参加する者がおり、時行および御内人の挙兵は結果的には御内人同士の戦いとなって鎌倉幕府再興の可能性を失わせるとともに、[[室町幕府]]や[[鎌倉府]]を支える吏僚層を形成するきっかけとなった{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=12-18}}{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=23-24}}。 時行は鎌倉を逃れた後も各地に潜伏し、南北朝成立後は吉野の[[南朝 (日本)|南朝]]から[[朝敵]]免除の[[綸旨]]を受けて南朝に従い、[[新田氏]]や[[北畠顕家]]の軍などに属して足利方と戦うが、[[正平 (日本)|正平]]7年/[[文和]]元年（[[1352年]]）足利方に捕縛され、翌年、鎌倉において処刑されたとされる。 == 題材にした作品 == * [[中先代の乱、始末 - 糾問歴史小説|中先代の乱、始末]]（[[清水米房]]、[[2008年]]、[[信毎書籍出版センター]]）{{Cite web |title=中先代の乱、始末 - 糾問歴史小説 |url=https://www.kinokuniya.co.jp/f/dsg-01-9784884110680 |website=紀伊國屋書店ウェブストア｜オンライン書店｜本、雑誌の通販、電子書籍ストア |access-date=2024-08-06 |language=ja}} * [[逃げ上手の若君]]（[[松井優征]]、[[2021年]]、[[集英社]]『[[週刊少年ジャンプ]]』連載） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|author=池永二郎|chapter=中先代の乱|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|volume=10|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|year=1989|isbn=978-4-642-00510-4}} * {{Citation|和書|author=小川信|chapter=中先代の乱|title=[[日本史大事典]]|volume=5|publisher=[[平凡社]]|year=1993|isbn=978-4-582-13105-5}} * {{Citation|和書|author=森茂暁|chapter=中先代の乱|title=[[日本歴史大事典]]|volume=3|publisher=[[小学館]]|year=2001|isbn=978-4-09-523003-0}} * {{Citation|和書|last=三浦|first=紀子|chapter=中先代の乱|title=日本中世史事典|publisher=[[朝倉書店]]|year=2008|isbn=978-4-254-53015-5}} * {{Citation|和書|last=阪田|first=雄一|chapter=中先代の乱と鎌倉将軍府|editor=佐藤博信|title=関東足利氏と東国社会|series=中世東国論:5|publisher=岩田書院|year=2012|isbn=978-4-87294-740-3}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本史の出来事一覧]] * [[日本の合戦一覧]] * [[観応の擾乱]] * [[青沼合戦]] == 外部リンク == * {{Kotobank}} {{デフォルトソート:なかせんたいのらん}} [[Category:日本の南北朝時代の戦い]] [[Category:相模国|戦なかせんたい]] [[Category:鎌倉市の歴史]] [[Category:信濃国|戦なかせんたい]] [[Category:長野県の歴史]] [[Category:1335年の日本]] revision.text:
{{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:Battles of the Nanboku-cho period]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Battles in Shinano Province]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Battles in 1335]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]]

However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:Battles of the Nanboku-cho period]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Battles in Shinano Province]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Battles in 1335]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]]

revision.comment: fixing the error I made with the apostrophe in the categories. Also fixing ills that were set to |en| instead of |ja| for some reason
revision.text:
{{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Battles of the Nanboku-cho period]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Battles in Shinano Province]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Battles in 1335]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Kk]][[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]]

revision.text:
{{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}} However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Battles of the Nanboku-cho period]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Battles in Shinano Province]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Battles in 1335]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]]

{{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Battles of the Nanboku-cho period]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Battles in Shinano Province]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Battles in 1335]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]]

{{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}} {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}} {{Battlebox | battle_name = 中先代の乱 | campaign = 延元の乱 |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = [[建武の乱]]（延元の乱） | date = [[建武 (日本)|建武]]2年（[[1335年]]）[[7月 (旧暦)|7月]] | place = 関東周辺（[[武蔵国]]、[[下野国]]、[[駿河国]]、[[遠江国]]、[[相模国]]） | result = 旧鎌倉幕府軍の鎌倉撤退 | combatant1 = [[ファイル:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[足利氏]] | combatant2 = [[ファイル:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[鎌倉幕府|旧鎌倉幕府軍]] | commander1 = [[ファイル:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[足利尊氏]]
[[ファイル:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[足利直義]] | commander2 = [[ファイル:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[北条時行]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]][[諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)|諏訪頼重]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''中先代の乱'''（なかせんだいのらん）は、[[建武 (日本)|建武]]2年（[[1335年]]）[[7月 (旧暦)|7月]]、[[北条高時]]（[[鎌倉幕府]]第14代[[執権]]）の遺児[[北条時行]]が、[[御内人]]の[[諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)|諏訪頼重]]らに擁立され、鎌倉幕府再興のため挙兵した反乱。北条時行は僅か10歳（諸説あり）の少年であったが[[足利直義]]を破り、鎌倉奪還に成功した。先代（[[北条氏]]）と後代（[[足利氏]]）との間にあって、一時的に鎌倉を支配したことから中先代の乱と呼ばれている。また、鎌倉支配が20日余りしか続かなかったことから、'''廿日先代'''（はつかせんだい）の異名もある{{Sfn|三浦|2008}}。 == 経過 == 鎌倉幕府滅亡後、[[建武の新政]]により、鎌倉には、後醍醐天皇の皇子・[[成良親王]]を長とし尊氏の弟の[[足利直義]]が執権としてこれを補佐する形の[[鎌倉将軍府]]が設置された。しかし建武政権は武家の支持を得られず、北条一族の残党などは各地で蜂起を繰り返していた。北条氏が守護を務めていた[[信濃国]]もその1つで、千曲川（[[信濃川]]）周辺ではたびたび蜂起が繰り返され、足利方の[[守護]][[小笠原貞宗]]らが鎮圧にあたっていた{{Sfn|阪田|2012|p=9}}。 [[建武 (日本)|建武]]2年（[[1335年]]）[[6月 (旧暦)|6月]]には、鎌倉時代に[[関東申次]]を務め、北条氏と繋がりがあった[[公家]]・[[西園寺公宗]]らが[[京都]]に潜伏していた北条高時の弟[[北条泰家]]（時興）を匿い、[[持明院統]]の[[光厳上皇]]を擁立して政権転覆を企てた陰謀が発覚する。公宗らは後醍醐天皇の[[暗殺]]に失敗して誅殺されたが、泰家は逃れ、各地の北条残党に挙兵を呼びかけた。 信濃に潜伏していた北条時行は、[[御内人]]であった[[諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)|諏訪頼重]]や[[滋野氏]]・[[仁科氏]]らに擁立されて挙兵した『[[梅松論]]』。時行の信濃挙兵に応じて[[北陸地方|北陸]]では北条一族の[[名越時兼]]が挙兵する。諏訪氏の縁続きに当たる保科弥三郎（[[保科氏]]）や四宮左衛門太郎（武水別神社神官家）、関屋氏、夏目氏ら時行勢は[[青沼合戦]]において[[船山守護所]]（小笠原貞宗）を襲撃した。この間に[[諏訪氏]]・滋野氏らは信濃国衙を焼き討ち襲撃して、建武政権が任命した公家の国司（[[清原氏 (広澄流)|清原]][[真人]]某）を自害させる『[[太平記]]』。 ところが、京都の建武政権は当初、反乱軍が時行を擁しているとの情報を掴んでいなかったらしく同年7月に信濃の豪族であった市河助房が京都に充てた[[着到状]]（[[市河文書]]所収「建武二年七月日市河助房着到状」）には、青沼合戦など反乱軍の動向については記されているが、反乱軍そのものについては「諏訪祝并滋野一族等依企謀叛」と記されており、時行の存在は知られていなかった。、京都では反乱軍は[[木曽谷|木曽路]]から[[尾張国]]に抜け、最終的には政権のある京都へと向かうと予想したために鎌倉将軍府への連絡が遅れ、それが後の鎌倉陥落につながったとみられている{{Sfn|阪田|2012|p=10}}。 勢いに乗った時行軍は[[武蔵国]]へ入り鎌倉に向けて進軍する。7月20日頃に女影原（[[埼玉県]][[日高市]]）で[[渋川義季]]や[[岩松経家]]らが率いる鎌倉将軍府の軍を、小手指ヶ原（同県[[所沢市]]）で[[今川範満 (南北朝時代)|今川範満]]の軍を、武蔵府中で救援に駆けつけた[[下野国]]守護・[[小山秀朝]]の軍を打ち破り、これらを自害あるいは討死させた。続いて、井手の沢（[[東京都]][[町田市]]）にて鎌倉から出陣して時行軍を迎撃した[[足利直義]]をも破る。直義は尊氏の子の幼い[[足利義詮]]や、後醍醐天皇の皇子[[成良親王]]らを連れて鎌倉を逃れる。 鎌倉には建武政権から失脚した後醍醐天皇の皇子[[護良親王]]（前征夷大将軍）が幽閉されていたが、直義は鎌倉を落ちる際に密かに家臣の[[淵辺義博]]に護良親王を殺害させている（7月23日）。鎌倉に護良を将軍・時行を執権とする鎌倉幕府が再興され建武政権に対抗する存在になることを恐れていたからと考えられている{{Sfn|阪田|2012|p=10}}。 7月24日、鎌倉将軍府側は鶴見（[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[鶴見区 (横浜市)|鶴見区]]）にて最後の抵抗を試みるが、[[佐竹義直 (南北朝時代)|佐竹義直]]（[[佐竹貞義]]の子）らが戦死、翌25日に時行は鎌倉に入り、一時的に支配する。更に時行勢は逃げる直義を、[[駿河国]]手越河原で撃破した。直義は乱の報告を京都に伝えると同時に成良親王を返還し、8月2日に[[三河国]]矢作に拠点を構えた。 時行勢の侵攻を知らされた建武政権では、足利尊氏が後醍醐天皇に対して、時行討伐の許可と同時に武家政権の設立に必要となる総追捕使と[[征夷大将軍]]の役職を要請するが、後醍醐天皇は要請を拒否する。 8月2日、尊氏は勅状を得ないまま出陣し、後醍醐天皇は尊氏に追って征東将軍の号を与える。尊氏は直義と合流し、9日に[[遠江国]]橋本（[[静岡県]][[湖西市]]）、12日に[[小夜の中山]]にて尊氏と同道してきた[[今川頼国]]の手により[[名越高邦]]戦死、14日に駿河国の[[清見関]]および国衙にて諏訪小二郎金刺頼秀（諏訪大社下社社家）が戦死、17日に[[相模国]][[箱根]]、18日に相模国[[相模川]]で尊氏方は義経来の北条方に遺恨を持つ[[中村経長]]が獅子奮迅の働きをするも中村沿革「中村郷土史」p.15.今川頼国・[[今川頼周|頼周]]兄弟が戦死するなど各地が激戦に見舞われた。時行勢は次第に劣勢となり戦線は徐々に後退。19日には相模国[[辻堂地区 (藤沢市)|辻堂]]で敗れた諏訪頼重が鎌倉[[勝長寿院]]で自害して、時行は鎌倉を保つこと20日余りで逃亡する。 後醍醐天皇は尊氏へ出陣の許可は与えなかったものの、8月30日の[[小山朝氏]]への[[下野国]]司兼守護への補任は尊氏の奏請に応じたものと考えられ、また間に合わなかったとは言え九州の[[大友貞載]]に出陣を求める[[綸旨]]を出していることから、少なくてもこの段階では尊氏と天皇の方針に大きな違いはなかったと考えられている。ところが、9月27日になり、尊氏は鎌倉において、乱の鎮圧に付き従った将士に勝手に恩賞の分配を行うため[[袖判下文]]を発給し、建武政権の上洛命令を無視したりするなど、建武政権から離反する（[[延元の乱]]）{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=12-13}}。 北条時行に従った武士には名越氏のような北条氏一門もいたが、大部分が諏訪頼重のような御内人であった。彼らの中には千葉氏・宇都宮氏・三浦氏などの関東有力武家の庶流の出身者が多数含まれており、結果的に関東武士が多数含まれることになった。これに対して佐竹氏・小山氏などの関東の御家人が鎌倉将軍府側に加わり、更に既に将軍府に出仕していた旧幕府吏僚や御内人の多くも時行の動きには従わずに、直義とともに鎌倉から脱出している。このため、鎌倉に入った時行は公式の幕府文書を発給することが出来ず、御内人のみで出せる得宗家の奉行人連署奉書で命令を下している。そして、建武政権に仕えていた旧幕府吏僚や御内人の中にも尊氏とともに時行討伐に参加する者がおり、時行および御内人の挙兵は結果的には御内人同士の戦いとなって鎌倉幕府再興の可能性を失わせるとともに、[[室町幕府]]や[[鎌倉府]]を支える吏僚層を形成するきっかけとなった{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=12-18}}{{Sfn|阪田|2012|pp=23-24}}。 時行は鎌倉を逃れた後も各地に潜伏し、南北朝成立後は吉野の[[南朝 (日本)|南朝]]から[[朝敵]]免除の[[綸旨]]を受けて南朝に従い、[[新田氏]]や[[北畠顕家]]の軍などに属して足利方と戦うが、[[正平 (日本)|正平]]7年/[[文和]]元年（[[1352年]]）足利方に捕縛され、翌年、鎌倉において処刑されたとされる。 == 題材にした作品 == * [[中先代の乱、始末 - 糾問歴史小説|中先代の乱、始末]]（[[清水米房]]、[[2008年]]、[[信毎書籍出版センター]]）{{Cite web |title=中先代の乱、始末 - 糾問歴史小説 |url=https://www.kinokuniya.co.jp/f/dsg-01-9784884110680 |website=紀伊國屋書店ウェブストア｜オンライン書店｜本、雑誌の通販、電子書籍ストア |access-date=2024-08-06 |language=ja}} * [[逃げ上手の若君]]（[[松井優征]]、[[2021年]]、[[集英社]]『[[週刊少年ジャンプ]]』連載） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related [[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related [[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related [[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}} However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}} {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}}, {{ill|Totomi Province|ja|遠江国}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Clan|ja|足利氏}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府|Old Kamakura Shogunate Forces}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hojo Tokiyuki|ja|北条時行}}, the child of {{ill|Hojo Takatoki|ja|北条高時}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|ja|建武の新政}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府}} was created under Prince {{ill|Nariyoshi|ja|成良親王}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|ja|後醍醐天皇}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|ja|小笠原貞宗}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Kenmu|ja|建武}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重 (南北朝時代)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|ja|武蔵国}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|ja|足利直義}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Morinaga|ja|護良親王}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|ja|足利尊氏}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|ja|逃げ上手の若君}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shonen Jump|ja|週刊少年ジャンプ}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史の出来事一覧}} * {{ill|List of Battles in Japanese History|ja|日本の合戦一覧}} * {{ill|Kanno Disturbance|ja|観応の擾乱}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦}}
