However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Battles of the Nanboku-cho period]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:Battles in Shinano Province]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]] [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[sn:akasendai Rebellion]] [[Category:1335年の戦闘]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Battles in Shinano Province
revision.text:
{{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|qq=}} (Engen Conflict) | date = [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ([[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]], [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Totomi Province]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]] | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]] | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the child of [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]] (14th Regent of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]), was supported by retainers such as [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]], the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]], a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|qq=}} was created under Prince [[:en:Prince Narinaga|Nariyoshi]] (son of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]]), supported by [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]]. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|qq=}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Battles of the Nanboku-cho period]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]] [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[sn:akasendai Rebellion]] [[Category:1335年の戦闘]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]]

Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]] [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[sn:akasendai Rebellion]] [[Category:1335年の戦闘]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:1335年の戦闘]] into [[Category:Battles of 1335]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|qq=}} (Engen Conflict) | date = [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ([[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]], [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Totomi Province]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]] | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]] | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the child of [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]] (14th Regent of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]), was supported by retainers such as [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]], the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]], a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|qq=}} was created under Prince [[:en:Prince Narinaga|Nariyoshi]] (son of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]]), supported by [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]]. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|qq=}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]] [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[sn:akasendai Rebellion]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]]

In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|qq=|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]] [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[sn:akasendai Rebellion]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10871756]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|qq=|1=Kenmu Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486475}} (Engen Conflict) | date = [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ([[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]], [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Totomi Province]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]] | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]] | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the child of [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]] (14th Regent of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]), was supported by retainers such as [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]], the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]], a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|qq=|1=Kamakura Governorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince [[:en:Prince Narinaga|Nariyoshi]] (son of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]]), supported by [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]]. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|qq=|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]]}} [[sn:Nakasendai Rebellion]] [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[sn:akasendai Rebellion]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10871756]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|qq=|1=Kenmu Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486475}} (Engen Conflict) | date = [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ([[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]], [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Totomi Province]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]] | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]] | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the child of [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]] (14th Regent of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]), was supported by retainers such as [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]], the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]], a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|qq=|1=Kamakura Governorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince [[:en:Prince Narinaga|Nariyoshi]] (son of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]]), supported by [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]]. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|qq=|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] }} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}}

[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the child of [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]] (14th Regent of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]), was supported by retainers such as [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] }} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}}

Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|qq=|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] }} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}}

[[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10871756]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|1=Kenmu Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486475}} (Engen Conflict) | date = [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ([[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]], [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Totomi Province]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]] | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]] | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the child of [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]] (14th Regent of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]), was supported by retainers such as [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]], the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]], a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|1=Kamakura Governorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince [[:en:Prince Narinaga|Nariyoshi]] (son of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]]), supported by [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]]. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] }} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}}

[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the child of [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]] (14th Regent of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]), was supported by retainers such as [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]], the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]], a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|1=Kamakura Governorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince [[:en:Prince Narinaga|Nariyoshi]] (son of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]]), supported by [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]]. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] }} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] }} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10871756]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|1=Kenmu Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486475}} (Engen Conflict) | date = [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ([[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]], [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Totomi Province]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]] | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]] | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the child of [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]] (14th Regent of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]), was supported by retainers such as [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]], the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]], a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|1=Kamakura Governorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince [[:en:Prince Narinaga|Nariyoshi]] (son of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]]), supported by [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]]. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] }} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10871756]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|1=Kenmu Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486475}} (Engen Conflict) | date = [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ([[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]], [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Totomi Province]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]] | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]] | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the child of [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]] (14th Regent of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]), was supported by retainers such as [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]], the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]], a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|1=Kamakura Governorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince [[:en:Prince Narinaga|Nariyoshi]] (son of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]]), supported by [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]]. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] }} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10871756]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|1=Kenmu Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486475}} (Engen Conflict) | date = [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ([[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]], [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Totomi Province]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]] | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]] | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]]
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]]{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when [[:en:Hōjō Tokiyuki|Hojo Tokiyuki]], the child of [[:en:Hōjō Takatoki|Hojo Takatoki]] (14th Regent of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]), was supported by retainers such as [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]], the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]], a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|1=Kamakura Governorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince [[:en:Prince Narinaga|Nariyoshi]] (son of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]]), supported by [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]]. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with [[:en:Ogasawara Sadamune|Ogasawara Sadamune]] tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of [[:en:Jianwu|Kenmu]] 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by [[:en:Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|Suwa Yorishige]] and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]], Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating [[:en:Ashikaga Tadayoshi|Ashikaga Tadayoshi]] at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated [[:en:Prince Moriyoshi|Prince Morinaga]] to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of [[:en:Ashikaga Takauji|Ashikaga Takauji]]'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] and [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]]. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * [[:en:The Elusive Samurai|The Elusive Samurai]] by Yusei Matsui, serialized in [[:en:Weekly Shōnen Jump|Weekly Shonen Jump]] (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:List of Japanese battles|List of Battles in Japanese History]] * [[:en:Kannō disturbance|Kanno Disturbance]] * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
