[[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10871756]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|1=Kenmu Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486475}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}}, {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Totomi Province}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Clan}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyuki|en|Hōjō Tokiyuki|WD=Q5965196|lt=Hojo Tokiyuki}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyuki|en|Hōjō Tokiyuki|WD=Q5965196|lt=Hojo Tokiyuki}}, the child of {{ill|Hōjō Takatoki|en|Hōjō Takatoki|WD=Q2355334|lt=Hojo Takatoki}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|1=Kamakura Governorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince {{ill|Prince Narinaga|en|Prince Narinaga|WD=Q2722113|lt=Nariyoshi}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|en|Ogasawara Sadamune|WD=Q7079835|lt=Ogasawara Sadamune}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Moriyoshi|en|Prince Moriyoshi|WD=Q2288858|lt=Prince Morinaga}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|en|The Elusive Samurai|WD=Q105100468|lt=The Elusive Samurai}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shōnen Jump|en|Weekly Shōnen Jump|WD=Q220015|lt=Weekly Shonen Jump}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Japanese battles|en|List of Japanese battles|WD=Q839176|lt=List of Battles in Japanese History}} * {{ill|Kannō disturbance|en|Kannō disturbance|WD=Q6364172|lt=Kanno Disturbance}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Moriyoshi|en|Prince Moriyoshi|WD=Q2288858|lt=Prince Morinaga}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|en|The Elusive Samurai|WD=Q105100468|lt=The Elusive Samurai}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shōnen Jump|en|Weekly Shōnen Jump|WD=Q220015|lt=Weekly Shonen Jump}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Japanese battles|en|List of Japanese battles|WD=Q839176|lt=List of Battles in Japanese History}} * {{ill|Kannō disturbance|en|Kannō disturbance|WD=Q6364172|lt=Kanno Disturbance}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|1=Kenmu Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486475}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}}, {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Totomi Province}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Clan}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyuki|en|Hōjō Tokiyuki|WD=Q5965196|lt=Hojo Tokiyuki}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyuki|en|Hōjō Tokiyuki|WD=Q5965196|lt=Hojo Tokiyuki}}, the child of {{ill|Hōjō Takatoki|en|Hōjō Takatoki|WD=Q2355334|lt=Hojo Takatoki}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|1=Kamakura Governorate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince {{ill|Prince Narinaga|en|Prince Narinaga|WD=Q2722113|lt=Nariyoshi}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|en|Ogasawara Sadamune|WD=Q7079835|lt=Ogasawara Sadamune}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Moriyoshi|en|Prince Moriyoshi|WD=Q2288858|lt=Prince Morinaga}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|en|The Elusive Samurai|WD=Q105100468|lt=The Elusive Samurai}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shōnen Jump|en|Weekly Shōnen Jump|WD=Q220015|lt=Weekly Shonen Jump}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Japanese battles|en|List of Japanese battles|WD=Q839176|lt=List of Battles in Japanese History}} * {{ill|Kannō disturbance|en|Kannō disturbance|WD=Q6364172|lt=Kanno Disturbance}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyuki|en|Hōjō Tokiyuki|WD=Q5965196|lt=Hojo Tokiyuki|1=Hōjō Tokiyuki}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyuki|en|Hōjō Tokiyuki|WD=Q5965196|lt=Hojo Tokiyuki|1=Hōjō Tokiyuki}}, the child of {{ill|Hōjō Takatoki|en|Hōjō Takatoki|WD=Q2355334|lt=Hojo Takatoki|1=Hōjō Takatoki}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration|1=Kenmu Restoration}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|1=Q11650890|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince {{ill|Prince Narinaga|en|Prince Narinaga|WD=Q2722113|lt=Nariyoshi|1=Prince Narinaga}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|en|Ogasawara Sadamune|WD=Q7079835|lt=Ogasawara Sadamune|1=Ogasawara Sadamune}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Moriyoshi|en|Prince Moriyoshi|WD=Q2288858|lt=Prince Morinaga|1=Prince Moriyoshi}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|en|The Elusive Samurai|WD=Q105100468|lt=The Elusive Samurai|1=The Elusive Samurai}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shōnen Jump|en|Weekly Shōnen Jump|WD=Q220015|lt=Weekly Shonen Jump|1=Weekly Shōnen Jump}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Japanese battles|en|List of Japanese battles|WD=Q839176|lt=List of Battles in Japanese History|1=List of Japanese battles}} * {{ill|Kannō disturbance|en|Kannō disturbance|WD=Q6364172|lt=Kanno Disturbance|1=Kannō disturbance}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|en|The Elusive Samurai|WD=Q105100468|lt=The Elusive Samurai|1=The Elusive Samurai}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shōnen Jump|en|Weekly Shōnen Jump|WD=Q220015|lt=Weekly Shonen Jump|1=Weekly Shōnen Jump}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Japanese battles|en|List of Japanese battles|WD=Q839176|lt=List of Battles in Japanese History|1=List of Japanese battles}} * {{ill|Kannō disturbance|en|Kannō disturbance|WD=Q6364172|lt=Kanno Disturbance|1=Kannō disturbance}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]][[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]][[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]][[ja:中先代の乱]][[ko:나카센다이의 난]][[zh:中先代之亂]] {{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|1=Q11486475|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486475}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}}, {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Clan|1=Ashikaga clan}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyuki|en|Hōjō Tokiyuki|WD=Q5965196|lt=Hojo Tokiyuki|1=Hōjō Tokiyuki}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyuki|en|Hōjō Tokiyuki|WD=Q5965196|lt=Hojo Tokiyuki|1=Hōjō Tokiyuki}}, the child of {{ill|Hōjō Takatoki|en|Hōjō Takatoki|WD=Q2355334|lt=Hojo Takatoki|1=Hōjō Takatoki}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration|1=Kenmu Restoration}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|1=Q11650890|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11650890}} was created under Prince {{ill|Prince Narinaga|en|Prince Narinaga|WD=Q2722113|lt=Nariyoshi|1=Prince Narinaga}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|en|Ogasawara Sadamune|WD=Q7079835|lt=Ogasawara Sadamune|1=Ogasawara Sadamune}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|WD=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|WD=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|WD=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Moriyoshi|en|Prince Moriyoshi|WD=Q2288858|lt=Prince Morinaga|1=Prince Moriyoshi}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|WD=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|en|The Elusive Samurai|WD=Q105100468|lt=The Elusive Samurai|1=The Elusive Samurai}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shōnen Jump|en|Weekly Shōnen Jump|WD=Q220015|lt=Weekly Shonen Jump|1=Weekly Shōnen Jump}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Japanese battles|en|List of Japanese battles|WD=Q839176|lt=List of Battles in Japanese History|1=List of Japanese battles}} * {{ill|Kannō disturbance|en|Kannō disturbance|WD=Q6364172|lt=Kanno Disturbance|1=Kannō disturbance}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中先代の乱]] [[fr:Rébellion_Nakasendai]] [[fa:شورش ناکاسندای]] [[fr:Rébellion Nakasendai]] [[id:Pemberontakan Nakasendai]] [[zh:中先代之亂]] {{wikidata link|Q10871756}} [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{Battlebox | battle_name = Nakasendai Rebellion | campaign = Engen Conflict |colour_scheme = background:#ffccaa | image = | caption = | conflict = {{ill|Kenmu Rebellion|ja|建武の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenmu Rebellion|1=Q11486475|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486475}} (Engen Conflict) | date = {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|qid=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} 2 (1335), 7th month (lunar calendar) | place = Regions around Kanto ({{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|qid=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}}, {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|qid=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}}, {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|qid=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|qid=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}) | result = Withdrawal of the Old Kamakura Shogunate forces from Kamakura | combatant1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|qid=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Clan|1=Ashikaga clan}} | combatant2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}} | commander1 = [[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}
[[File:Ashikaga mon.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|qid=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} | commander2 = [[File:Japanese Crest mitu Uroko.svg|25x20px]] {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyuki|en|Hōjō Tokiyuki|qid=Q5965196|lt=Hojo Tokiyuki|1=Hōjō Tokiyuki}}
[[File:Japanese crest Suwa Kajinoha(White background).svg|20px]] {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|qid=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)}}{{KIA}} | strength1 = | strength2 = | casualties1 = | casualties2 = }} '''Nakasendai Rebellion''' (中先代の乱) occurred in {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|qid=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} 2 (1335) during the 7th lunar month when {{ill|Hōjō Tokiyuki|en|Hōjō Tokiyuki|qid=Q5965196|lt=Hojo Tokiyuki|1=Hōjō Tokiyuki}}, the child of {{ill|Hōjō Takatoki|en|Hōjō Takatoki|qid=Q2355334|lt=Hojo Takatoki|1=Hōjō Takatoki}} (14th Regent of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}), was supported by retainers such as {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|qid=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)}} to rebel in an attempt to restore the Kamakura Shogunate. Though Tokiyuki, aged around 10 (according to some accounts), initially succeeded in recapturing Kamakura by defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|qid=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}}, the Kamakura control lasted for only 20 days, earning it the alternate name "Hatsuka Sendai" (廿日先代). == Course of Events == After the Kamakura Shogunate's fall and the establishment of the {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|qid=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration|1=Kenmu Restoration}}, a {{ill|Kamakura Governorate|ja|鎌倉将軍府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Governorate|1=Q11650890|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11650890}} was created under Prince {{ill|Prince Narinaga|en|Prince Narinaga|qid=Q2722113|lt=Nariyoshi|1=Prince Narinaga}} (son of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|qid=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}), supported by {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|qid=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}}. However, the Kenmu government failed to garner support among warriors, and remnants of the Hojo family repeatedly rebelled in various regions. In {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|qid=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, uprisings were particularly frequent along the {{ill|Chikuma River|ja|千曲川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma River|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃川|1=Shinano River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q734455}} (Shinano River), with {{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|en|Ogasawara Sadamune|qid=Q7079835|lt=Ogasawara Sadamune|1=Ogasawara Sadamune}} tasked with quelling them. In the 6th month of {{ill|Jianwu|en|Jianwu|qid=Q11065423|lt=Kenmu|1=Jianwu}} 2 (1335), a conspiracy led by court noble {{ill|Saionji Kinmune|ja|西園寺公宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saionji Kinmune|1=Saionji Kinmune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7223159}} to reinstate the Hojo family's rule by assassinating Emperor Go-Daigo failed, resulting in Kinmune's execution. Hojo Tokiyuki, sheltered in Shinano, was rallied by {{ill|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|en|Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)|qid=Q11631966|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige (Nanboku-chō period)}} and other supporters to launch a rebellion. This included attacks on the local provincial office and defeating the forces of the Kamakura Governorate in battles such as the {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11662585}}. Advancing through {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, Tokiyuki's forces decisively defeated the Kamakura Governorate's armies at key locations, including defeating {{ill|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|en|Ashikaga Tadayoshi|qid=Q725375|lt=Ashikaga Tadayoshi|1=Ashikaga Tadayoshi}} at Ide no Sawa. Upon retreating, Tadayoshi assassinated {{ill|Prince Moriyoshi|en|Prince Moriyoshi|qid=Q2288858|lt=Prince Morinaga|1=Prince Moriyoshi}} to prevent his potential rise as a rival power in Kamakura. Eventually, Tokiyuki captured Kamakura on the 25th of the 7th lunar month. However, the arrival of {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}'s reinforcements led to fierce battles in {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|qid=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}} and {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|qid=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province|1=Sagami Province}}. By the 19th day of the 8th lunar month, Suwa Yorishige perished, and Tokiyuki fled, marking the end of his brief control over Kamakura. == Cultural Depictions == * *The Nakasendai Rebellion* by {{ill|Shimizu Komeibo|ja|清水米房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu Komeibo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (2008) * {{ill|The Elusive Samurai|en|The Elusive Samurai|qid=Q105100468|lt=The Elusive Samurai|1=The Elusive Samurai}} by Yusei Matsui, serialized in {{ill|Weekly Shōnen Jump|en|Weekly Shōnen Jump|qid=Q220015|lt=Weekly Shonen Jump|1=Weekly Shōnen Jump}} (2021–) == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Japanese Historical Events|ja|日本史のEvents一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese Historical Events|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Japanese battles|en|List of Japanese battles|qid=Q839176|lt=List of Battles in Japanese History|1=List of Japanese battles}} * {{ill|Kannō disturbance|en|Kannō disturbance|qid=Q6364172|lt=Kanno Disturbance|1=Kannō disturbance}} * {{ill|Battle of Aonuma|ja|青沼合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Aonuma|1=Battle of Aonuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11662585}} == External Links == * {{Kotobank}} {{translated page|ja|中先代の乱|version=103865906|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q10871756|fa|شورش ناکاسندای|fr|Rébellion Nakasendai|id|Pemberontakan Nakasendai|ja|中先代の乱|ko|나카센다이의 난|zh|中先代之亂|fr|Rébellion_Nakasendai}} [[Category:1335 in Japan]] [[Category:Ashikaga Takauji]] [[Category:Battles in the Nambokucho period]] [[Category:Battles of 1335]] [[Category:History of Kamakura]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sagami Province]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
